title: 10 best herbal teas for gum infection
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum infection are infusions made from plants and herbs that have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and soothing properties, which can help to reduce gum inflammation, fight infection, and promote healing. Examples of herbal teas that can help with gum infection include chamomile, calendula, echinacea, and sage. These herbs contain compounds that can help to reduce inflammation, fight bacteria, and promote tissue regeneration. By drinking these herbal teas, people with gum infection can experience relief from pain and discomfort, reduce the risk of further infection, and promote the healing of gum tissue. This can lead to improved oral health, fresher breath, and a better quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with gum infection because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth that can cause gum infection. Additionally, sage tea can reduce inflammation and soothe irritation in the gums, promoting healing and preventing further infection. Drinking sage tea or using it as a mouthwash can help alleviate the symptoms of gum infection and improve overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Sage tea has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria, reducing the risk of gum infection and promoting oral health. This is due to the presence of active constituents such as camphor, camphene, and cineole.

Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, providing relief from gum infection symptoms. This is attributed to the presence of rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Sage tea contains powerful antioxidants that help protect gum tissue from damage caused by free radicals, promoting gum health. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid, ellagic acid, and various flavonoids.

Astringent: Sage tea has astringent properties that help tighten and tone gum tissue, reducing the risk of gum infection and promoting gum health. This is due to the presence of tannins and volatile oils.

Analgesic: Sage tea has analgesic properties that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gum infection. This is attributed to the presence of active constituents such as camphor, camphene, and cineole.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried sage leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves.
Allow the tea to cool to a drinkable temperature before sipping.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the leaves help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria responsible for gum infections.
Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, the roots help soothe irritated gums and promote gum tissue healing.
Bark: Packed with tannins, the bark aids in reducing gum inflammation and has an astringent effect on the mouth's mucous membranes.
Flowers: High in antioxidants, the flowers help protect gum tissues from damage caused by free radicals and support overall oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with gum infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, can help to soothe irritated gums and promote healing. Additionally, pot marigold tea has been shown to have astringent properties that can help to tighten and strengthen gum tissue, reducing the risk of further infection. Drinking pot marigold tea regularly may help to maintain good oral health and prevent gum disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce gum inflammation due to its high content of anti-inflammatory flavonoids, like luteolin and quercetin.
Antimicrobial: The tea's antimicrobial properties, derived from active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils, inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria causing gum infections.
Wound-healing: Pot marigold tea promotes gum tissue regeneration and wound healing, thanks to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory components, such as coumarins and carotenoids.
Analgesic: The tea's natural analgesic properties, attributed to its flavonoid and volatile oil content, help alleviate gum pain and discomfort associated with gum infection.
Antiseptic: Pot marigold tea's antiseptic properties, due to the presence of active compounds like flavonoids and tannins, aid in preventing gum infection and further complications.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried pot marigold flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the flowers in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the flowers.
Drink the tea, allowing it to soothe and heal gum infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, flowers help reduce inflammation and promote gum healing.
Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making leaves effective for treating gum infections.
Seeds: High in linoleic acid, seeds can reduce inflammation and support gum tissue regeneration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with gum infection because it contains powerful antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can effectively kill the bacteria causing gum infection. The thymol compound found in thyme tea inhibits the growth of periodontal pathogens, reducing inflammation, swelling, and bleeding of the gums. Additionally, thyme tea's antioxidant properties promote healing and tissue regeneration, supporting overall oral health. Drinking thyme tea or using it as a mouthwash can provide relief from gum infection and prevent its recurrence.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Thyme tea contains thymol, which is an antibacterial agent that helps reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for gum infections.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of flavonoids, which help reduce gum inflammation caused by infection.
Antioxidant: Thyme tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help protect gum tissues from damage caused by free radicals during infection.
Analgesic: Thyme tea contains compounds with analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gum infections.
Antifungal: Thyme tea has antifungal properties, which help prevent the growth of fungi that can exacerbate gum infections.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves or 1 tablespoon of fresh thyme leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the tea simmer for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the thyme leaves.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking, ideally at a warm temperature. Sip slowly, swishing the tea around in your mouth before swallowing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antiseptic and antibacterial compounds, making them effective for treating gum infections.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain healing properties that can help reduce inflammation and promote gum tissue recovery.
Stems: Thyme stems have antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate the bacteria causing gum infections.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with gum infection because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea is made from the flower heads of the eastern purple coneflower plant, which is rich in antioxidants and other beneficial compounds. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation in the gums, help to kill harmful bacteria in the mouth, and promote overall oral health. Drinking eastern purple coneflower tea may help to soothe irritated gums, reduce redness and swelling, and prevent the progression of gum infection. Regular use of this tea may also help to boost the immune system and improve overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tea reduces gum inflammation, helping to alleviate pain and swelling, due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like chrysins and eudesmanolides.
Antimicrobial: The tea inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria, preventing the spread of gum infection, thanks to its rich content of antimicrobial agents like alkamides and flavonoids.

Wound-healing: Eastern purple coneflower tea promotes gum tissue regeneration, supporting the healing process, by containing active constituents such as isobutylamides and essential oils that stimulate tissue repair.
Analgesic: The tea provides pain relief for gum infection by containing analgesic compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids, which help to minimize discomfort.

Immune-boosting: Eastern purple coneflower tea strengthens the immune system, enhancing the body's ability to fight off gum infection, as it is abundant in immune-enhancing constituents like polysaccharides and alkylamides.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 dried purple coneflower flower heads, ensuring they were grown in an eastern region.
Chop or grind the coneflower heads to increase the surface area for steeping.
Place 1 teaspoon of chopped coneflower in a tea infuser or empty tea bag.
Steep the coneflower in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then remove the infuser or tea bag.
Drink the prepared tea, ideally 2-3 times a day, for gum infection relief.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating gum infections.
Leaves: Contain beneficial compounds that help reduce inflammation and fight harmful bacteria in the mouth.
Roots: Packed with medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and analgesic effects, useful for soothing gum infections and alleviating pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with gum infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. This herbal remedy may help soothe irritated gums, reduce redness, and promote overall oral health. Drinking St. John's wort tea regularly can help maintain a healthy oral environment and prevent gum infections. However, it is essential to consult a dental professional for proper diagnosis and treatment of gum infections.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: St. John's wort tea has antibacterial properties that help reduce the number of bacteria causing gum infection, thanks to its active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help decrease gum inflammation and swelling, reducing the discomfort and promoting healing, due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.
Analgesic: St. John's wort tea can help alleviate gum pain, as it contains active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin, which have pain-relieving properties.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of St. John's wort tea, derived from its active constituents such as flavonoids and hypericin, help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and support overall oral health.

Antiviral: St. John's wort tea's antiviral properties, attributed to active constituents like hypericin and pseudohypericin, can help prevent the spread of viral infections in the gums, contributing to overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried St. John's Wort herb.
Heat the mixture on high until it reaches boiling point, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the spent herb material.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, gently swishing it around the mouth before swallowing, for 5 to 10 minutes after meals.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, St. John's Wort flowers are used to make medicinal teas that help reduce gum inflammation and fight infections.
Leaves: Containing tannins and flavonoids, St. John's Wort leaves are used in teas to soothe gum irritation, promote healing, and reduce bacterial growth.

Aerial parts: Although not a specific part, aerial parts (stems and leaves) of St. John's Wort are used in some preparations for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, aiding in gum infection treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog rose tea helps with gum infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The tea is rich in vitamin C and bioflavonoids, which can strengthen the gums and promote healing. Additionally, the astringent properties of dog rose tea can help tighten gum tissue, reducing the pockets where bacteria can accumulate. Drinking dog rose tea regularly may help prevent and treat gum infections, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dog rose tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of polyphenols and anthocyanins, helps reduce gum inflammation caused by infection.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial effect of dog rose tea, attributed to its vitamin C and bioflavonoid content, aids in controlling gum infection by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria.
Antioxidant: Dog rose tea's high antioxidant property, resulting from its rich polyphenol content, supports gum health by neutralizing free radicals and promoting tissue repair.
Astringent: The astringent property of dog rose tea, owing to its tannin content, helps tighten gum tissues, reduce gum bleeding, and protect gums from further infection.
Analgesic: Dog rose tea's analgesic effect, attributed to its flavonoid content, alleviates gum pain and discomfort associated with gum infection.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 teaspoon of dried dog rose petals in 1 cup of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture to remove petals.
Add raw honey to taste, if desired.
Let the tea cool down to a warm temperature.
Rinse your dog's mouth with the tea, gently swishing it around the gums for 30 seconds. Repeat up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Dog rose flowers contain antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for soothing inflamed gums.
Leaves: Dog rose leaves are rich in tannins, which can help reduce gum inflammation and promote healing.
Bark: The bark of the dog rose plant contains astringent compounds that can help reduce gum inflammation and strengthen gum tissue.
Buds: Dog rose buds are a rich source of vitamin C and other antioxidants, which can support gum health and boost the immune system.
Seeds: Dog rose seeds contain essential fatty acids and are a good source of vitamin E, which can help reduce inflammation and promote gum tissue regeneration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with gum infection because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The active compounds in licorice, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of periodontal pathogens that cause gum disease. Additionally, licorice tea can help soothe and heal gum tissue, promoting overall oral health. Drinking licorice tea regularly may help prevent gum infections and maintain healthy gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's ability to reduce inflammation is important for gum infection as it helps alleviate pain, swelling, and redness in the gums. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: Licorice tea's antimicrobial properties help fight the bacteria causing gum infection, thus preventing the infection from spreading and worsening. The key active constituents responsible are glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and chalcones.

Antiviral: Licorice tea's antiviral properties help protect the gums from viral infections that can exacerbate gum issues. Glycyrrhizin is the primary active constituent that contributes to this property.

Antioxidant: Licorice tea's antioxidant properties help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by infection. The active constituents that contribute to this property include flavonoids and isoflavonoids.

Analgesic: Licorice tea's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by gum infection. The active constituents responsible for this property are glycyrrhizin and certain flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove licorice root pieces.
Allow the tea to cool down until it's warm, but not hot.
Sip the tea slowly, holding it in your mouth near the infected gum area before swallowing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and anti-viral properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and fight oral infections.
Rhizomes: Similar to roots, licorice rhizomes have glycyrrhizin and other beneficial compounds that can soothe irritated gums and promote oral health.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains flavonoids and coumarins that exhibit antimicrobial activities, which can be useful in treating gum infections.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are rich in antioxidants and have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including gum infections, due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry tea helps with gum infection because it contains potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and fight infection. The tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C, that support oral health by boosting the immune system and promoting tissue repair. Moreover, elderberry tea has antibacterial and antiviral properties that inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for gum infections. Regular consumption of elderberry tea can alleviate gum inflammation, prevent gum disease, and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces gum inflammation, helping to alleviate pain and promote healing. This property is due to the presence of anthocyanins and other flavonoids.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants in elderberry tea, such as anthocyanins and vitamin C, help protect gum tissue from damage caused by oxidative stress and support overall oral health.

Antibacterial: Elderberry tea's antibacterial properties inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria, reducing the risk of gum infection and promoting oral health. Active constituents responsible for this property include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other polyphenols.

Immune-boosting: Elderberry tea supports the immune system, helping the body fight off gum infections and promoting overall oral health. Key immune-boosting compounds in elderberry include anthocyanins, vitamin C, and other antioxidants.

Analgesic: Elderberry tea's analgesic properties help alleviate gum pain and discomfort associated with gum infection. This property is due to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including anthocyanins and other polyphenols.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried elderberries.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the solid elderberry pieces.
Add honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired.
Sip the warm tea, allowing it to gently soothe and disinfect the gums.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Elderberry flowers contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and swelling.
Bark: The bark of elderberry plants has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the bacteria causing gum infections.
Leaves: Elderberry leaves contain tannins that can help soothe inflamed gums and promote healing.
Berries: Elderberries are rich in antioxidants that can help boost the immune system and speed up the healing process.
Stems: Elderberry stems contain flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tea helps with gum infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The tea is made from the leaves of the plantain herb, which contain tannins, flavonoids, and other compounds that can help to soothe irritated gums and promote healing. Drinking plantain tea or using it as a mouthwash can help to reduce pain and swelling, prevent the spread of infection, and promote overall oral health. Additionally, plantain tea is a natural and gentle alternative to chemical-based mouthwashes and treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces gum inflammation, helping to alleviate pain and swelling, due to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.
Antimicrobial: Plantain tea's antimicrobial property inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria, helping to prevent and treat gum infections, thanks to the action of tannins, flavonoids, and saponins.
Astringent: Plantain tea's astringent property helps tighten and heal gum tissues, reducing the risk of gum infection, because of the presence of tannins.
Analgesic: Plantain tea's analgesic property alleviates gum pain and discomfort, promoting faster healing, owing to the presence of various flavonoids and triterpenoids.

Antioxidant: Plantain tea's antioxidant property helps protect gum tissues from damage, supporting overall oral health, due to the action of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other antioxidant compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest two large plantain leaves (Plantago major) from a chemical-free area.
Rinse leaves thoroughly, then tear into small pieces and place in a pot.
Add 4 cups of water to the pot, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the liquid, discarding the leaves, and let the tea cool to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1/2 cup of the plantain tea, three times a day, to help soothe gum infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Plantain leaves are often used in medicinal teas for gum infection due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Seeds: Plantain seeds can be used in teas to help reduce pain and inflammation in the gums, as they contain mucilage that can help soothe irritated tissues.
Bark: The bark of the plantain plant can be used in teas to fight gum infection, as it contains compounds with antimicrobial and astringent properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with gum infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The tea is made from the leaves of the marshmallow plant, which contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective layer on the mucous membranes, providing relief from pain and promoting healing. Additionally, marshmallow tea has been shown to reduce the production of prostaglandins, compounds that cause inflammation in the body. By reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria, marshmallow tea can help to alleviate the symptoms of gum infection and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage that forms a protective film on the gums, reducing pain and inflammation, which is important for gum infection as it soothes and promotes healing of irritated gum tissues.

Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce swelling and redness in gums, which is crucial for gum infection as it alleviates discomfort and fosters tissue recovery.

Antibacterial property: Marshmallow tea includes tannins and other antibacterial agents that help inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria causing gum infection, making it important for gum infection as it helps control and prevent further infection.

Antioxidant property: Marshmallow tea is rich in antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage, which is vital for gum infection as it aids in preventing tissue degeneration and promotes recovery.

Analgesic property: Marshmallow tea contains compounds like triterpene saponins that have analgesic effects, which is significant for gum infection as it helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gum infection.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried marshmallow root in the hot water for 10-15 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea root and 1 teaspoon of goldenseal root to the marshmallow tea.
Steep all ingredients together for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it, up to three times a day for gum infection treatment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, marshmallow leaves are used to soothe inflamed gums and fight oral infections.
Roots: Containing high levels of mucilage, marshmallow roots are used to make teas that can help reduce gum inflammation and promote tissue repair.
Flowers: With antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, marshmallow flowers can be used in teas to help alleviate gum pain and reduce redness.
Bark: The bark of the marshmallow plant contains soothing mucilage, making it suitable for gargling to relieve gum infection symptoms.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum infection with herbal teas is to use a strong infusion of antimicrobial herbs, such as sage, calendula, or echinacea. Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried herb in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then rinse your mouth with the cooled tea several times a day. Regular use can reduce inflammation, fight infection, and promote healing. However, it's important to also maintain good oral hygiene and seek professional dental care if symptoms persist.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal teas include inflammation and soreness of the mouth and throat. Herbal teas with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, such as chamomile, sage, and licorice root, can help alleviate symptoms of gum infection and other mouth-related ailments. Chamomile tea has soothing properties that can reduce inflammation and provide relief from pain, while sage tea has antimicrobial and astringent properties that can help heal wounds and reduce infection. Licorice root tea has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help relieve pain and inflammation in the mouth. However, it is important to note that while herbal teas can provide relief for minor ailments, they should not be used as a substitute for professional medical or dental treatment.
